title_en: Al-Mawardi and the Ethics of the Two Abodes
content_id:
Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din — Etika Dunia Ini dan Agama — adalah salah satu karya etika Islam yang paling praktis yang pernah ditulis. Penulisnya, Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad al-Mawardi (972-1058 M), adalah ahli fikih Syafi'i utama, teoritikus politik, dan penulis karya-karya yang mencakup yurisprudensi Islam, filsafat politik, dan teologi moral. Ia menjabat sebagai qadi agung (hakim kepala) kekhalifahan Abbasiyah dan merupakan salah satu ulama paling berpengaruh di zamannya. Mahakarya politiknya Al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah (Hukum-Hukum Pemerintahan) adalah salah satu teks dasar teori politik Islam.

Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din mencerminkan keyakinan al-Mawardi bahwa etika perilaku yang tepat tidak dapat dibagi menjadi kategori keagamaan murni dan duniawi murni — melainkan keduanya membentuk satu kesatuan yang utuh. Judul buku ini mengungkapkan integrasi ini: etika Islam mengatur kedua dimensi kehidupan manusia, dan Muslim yang berperilaku sesuai dengan prinsip-prinsip Islam dalam urusan duniawinya sekaligus memenuhi kewajibannya agamanya. Buku ini ditulis untuk membimbing umat Islam dalam etika praktis kehidupan sehari-hari dengan cara yang melayani kesejahteraan duniawi mereka maupun tujuan akhir mereka di akhirat.

Pendekatan al-Mawardi bersifat empiris dan praktis daripada murni teoretis. Ia mengandalkan ayat-ayat al-Quran, hadis kenabian, kebijaksanaan orang-orang Arab dan Persia pra-Islam, serta wawasan-wawasan para filsuf Yunani sebagaimana dimediasi melalui keilmuan Islam untuk membangun gambaran komprehensif tentang kebijaksanaan praktis. Kesediaannya untuk mengambil dari berbagai sumber kebijaksanaan mencerminkan orientasi intelektual yang luas yang menjadi ciri tradisi keilmuan Abbasiyah pada puncaknya.

Buku ini disusun di sekitar lima tema utama: akal dan nilainya, perilaku pribadi dan kemuliaan karakter, etika interaksi sosial dan hubungan antarmanusia, etika tata kelola dan kehidupan publik, serta sikap yang tepat terhadap ketidakpastian kehidupan duniawi. Organisasi ini menjadikan karya ini sebagai panduan etika praktis yang benar-benar komprehensif yang mencakup keseluruhan eksistensi sosial seorang Muslim.
content_en:
Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din — Ethics of This World and Religion — is one of the most practically oriented works of Islamic ethics ever written. Its author, Abu al-Hasan Ali ibn Muhammad al-Mawardi (972–1058 CE), was a major Shafi'i jurist, political theorist, and author of works spanning Islamic jurisprudence, political philosophy, and moral theology. He served as the supreme qadi (chief judge) of the Abbasid caliphate and was one of the most influential scholars of his age. His political masterwork Al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah (The Laws of Governance) is one of the foundational texts of Islamic political theory.

Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din reflects al-Mawardi's conviction that the ethics of proper conduct cannot be divided into purely religious and purely worldly categories — rather, they form a seamless whole. The book's title expresses this integration: Islamic ethics governs both dimensions of human life, and the Muslim who conducts himself according to Islamic principles in his worldly affairs is simultaneously fulfilling his religious obligations. The book was written to guide Muslims in the practical ethics of daily life in a way that serves both their worldly welfare and their ultimate destination in the hereafter.

Al-Mawardi's approach is empirical and practical rather than purely theoretical. He draws on Quranic verses, prophetic hadiths, the wisdom of the pre-Islamic Arabs and Persians, and the insights of Greek philosophers as mediated through Islamic scholarship to construct a comprehensive account of practical wisdom. His willingness to draw on diverse sources of wisdom reflects a broad intellectual orientation characteristic of the Abbasid scholarly tradition at its peak.

The book is organized around five major themes: reason and its value, personal conduct and character virtues, the ethics of social interaction and relationships, the ethics of governance and public life, and the proper attitude toward the uncertainties of worldly life. This organization makes the work a genuinely comprehensive guide to practical ethics that covers the full range of a Muslim's social existence.
content_ar: Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din — Ethics of This الدنيا and Religion — is one of the most practically oriented works of Islamic ethics ever written. Its author, Abu al-حسن علي ibn محمد ﷺ al-Mawardi (972–1058 CE), was a major الشافعي jurist, political theorist, and author of works spanning Islamic jurisprudence, political philosophy, and moral theology. He served as the supreme qadi (chief judge) of the Abbasid الخلافة and was one of the most influential العلماء of his age. His political masterwork Al-Ahkam as-Sultaniyyah (The Laws of Governance) is one of the foundational texts of Islamic political theory. Adab ad-Dunya wad-Din reflects al-Mawardi's conviction that the ethics of proper السلوك cannot be divided into purely religious and purely worldly categories — rather, they form a seamless whole. The الكتاب's title expresses this integration: Islamic ethics governs both dimensions of human الحياة, and the مسلم who conducts himself وفقاً لـ Islamic principles in his worldly affairs is simultaneously fulfilling his religious obligations. The الكتاب was written to guide المسلمون in the practical ethics of daily الحياة in a الطريق that serves both their worldly welfare and their ultimate destination in الآخرة. Al-Mawardi's approach is empirical and practical rather than purely theoretical. He draws on القرآني الآيات, prophetic hadiths, the الحكمة of the pre-Islamic Arabs and Persians, and the insights of Greek philosophers as mediated through Islamic scholarship to construct a comprehensive account of practical الحكمة. His willingness to draw on diverse sources of الحكمة reflects a broad intellectual orientation characteristic of the Abbasid العلمي التراث at its peak. The الكتاب is organized around five major themes: العقل and its value, personal السلوك and الأخلاق الفضائل, the ethics of social interaction and relationships, the ethics of governance and public الحياة, and the proper attitude toward the uncertainties of worldly الحياة. This organization makes the work a genuinely comprehensive guide to practical ethics that covers the full range of a مسلم's social existence.
